name: Baked Potato Enchiladas on Ancho Ranchero Sauce with Pico De Gallo Corn and Mexican Tortilla Salad
recipeIngredient: 3 to 4 scallions, green parts only, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Bake potatoes until tender, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
3. Cool the baked potatoes and peel them.
4. Dice the cooled potatoes.
5. Combine the diced potatoes with grated Jalapeno Jack cheese, chopped scallions, cilantro, lime juice, sour cream, and salt in a bowl. Mix thoroughly and season to taste. Chill the mixture.
6. Heat 1/4 inch of canola oil in a skillet over medium heat.
7. Fry each corn tortilla for about 30 seconds on each side, just until they bubble and soften. Remove with tongs and pat off excess oil with paper towels.
8. Divide the potato filling evenly between the warm tortillas and roll up tightly. Place the rolled tortillas seam-side down on a greased baking sheet.
9. Bake the enchiladas for 15 minutes in the preheated oven.
10. Serve 2 enchiladas per person, topped with Ancho Ranchero Sauce, garnished with Pico de Gallo Corn.
